Name: Museo del Prado
Address: C/ de Ruiz de Alarcón 23 28014 Madrid
Tips: Spain's national art museum — one of the world's greatest. Highlights: Velázquez Las Meninas (Room 12) Goya Black Paintings (Rooms 67–68) Bosch Garden of Earthly Delights (Room 56A) El Greco. Under-18s always free. Book timed entry 2–4 weeks ahead in August/September.
Family: Excellent — under 18 always free; audio guide turns rooms into stories

Name: Museo Reina Sofía
Tips: Home to Picasso's Guernica — the defining anti-war artwork of the 20th century. Painted 1937 in response to bombing of Basque town. Also major Dalí (The Great Masturbator Girl at the Window) Miró Tàpies. Jean Nouvel glass tower extension (2005). Read about Guernica before going. Closed Tuesdays.

Name: Museo Thyssen-Bornemisza
Tips: Third pillar of the Paseo del Arte triangle. Bridges Prado (pre-19th) and Reina Sofía (20th). Bought from Baron Thyssen-Bornemisza in 1993 for ~$350M. Flemish primitives German Renaissance Impressionists Pop Art (Hopper Lichtenstein). Less crowded than the Prado.

Name: Palacio Real
Cost Range: €14 (under 5 free; free Wed/Thu 17:00–19:00 for EU citizens)
Tips: Official Spanish royal residence (state ceremonies only). One of Europe's largest palaces — 3418 rooms 135000m². Built 1738–1764 over Moorish Alcázar. The Royal Armoury has the finest collection of European royal armour in the world — full Hapsburg suits jousting harnesses child-sized parade armour. Free Jardines de Sabatini adjacent. Changing of the Guard Wed + Sat 11:00–14:00. Arrive 10:00.

Name: Catedral de la Almudena
Tips: Madrid's neo-Gothic cathedral — built 1879–1993 consecrated by John Paul II (first papal cathedral consecration outside Rome). Exterior matches Palacio Real opposite. Interior is shockingly contemporary — coloured stained glass brightly painted Gothic vaults Marko Rupnik mosaics. The crypt below (separate entrance C/ Mayor) is the largest Neo-Romanesque crypt in Spain (400 columns) — a hidden marvel.

Name: Parque del Retiro
Tips: Madrid's great central park — 118 hectares formal gardens tree-lined promenades Estanque lake with rowboats glass Palacio de Cristal (1887) brick Palacio de Velázquez. UNESCO-listed since 2021. Most beloved space in Madrid. Rent rowboats on the Estanque (€6–8) — iconic Madrid family activity. Free weekend puppet theatre.

Name: Plaza Mayor
Tips: 17th-century arcaded square built 1617–19 under Felipe III (equestrian statue at centre). 9 archways into the labyrinth around it. North side: Casa de la Panadería with painted frescoes. Site of bullfights public executions autos-da-fé. Go 08:00 before crowds. Side-street cafés 30% cheaper than arcade cafés. Sunday morning stamp-and-coin market.

Name: Puerta del Sol
Tips: Madrid's geographic and symbolic centre — Kilómetro 0 the marker for all Spanish national roads (in pavement in front of Casa de Correos). Famous New Year's Eve clock tower. The Bear and Strawberry Tree (El Oso y el Madroño) is the classic Madrid selfie. Pickpocket alert.

Name: El Rastro
Address: Calle de la Ribera de Curtidores La Latina 28005 Madrid
Tips: Legendary Sunday-morning street market running since the 16th century — 3500+ stalls. Sundays + holidays only 09:00–15:00. Arrive 09:00; by 11:00 impossibly crowded. Side streets (Calle del Carnero vinyl Mira el Río Alta military memorabilia) have specialist dealers. Combine with La Latina tapas. Pickpockets aggressive — front-pocket only.
Family: Good — treasure hunt for kids; watch bags carefully

Name: Templo de Debod
Address: C/ Ferraz s/n (Pg. del Pintor Rosales) 28008 Madrid
Cost Range: Free (exterior); interior free with timed ticket
Tips: Genuine 2nd-century BC Egyptian temple dedicated to Amun. Donated to Spain by Egypt 1968 (thanks for help saving temples threatened by Aswan Dam). Relocated stone by stone reassembled in Parque del Oeste. Reflecting pools west-facing terrace for sunset over Casa de Campo + Guadarrama mountains. THE view of the trip. Arrive 45 min before sunset (~20:18 in Sep).

Name: Teleférico de Madrid
Address: Paseo del Pintor Rosales s/n 28008 Madrid
Tips: 2.5km cable car from Parque del Oeste over the Manzanares river into Casa de Campo. 11 minutes max altitude 40m panoramic views over Palacio Real + Almudena + Debod + Guadarrama mountains. Classic Madrid kid-and-grandparent activity since 1969. Sat–Sun + holidays from 12:00; weekday hours seasonal — verify.

Name: Basílica de San Francisco el Grande
Address: Plaza de San Francisco s/n 28005 Madrid (La Latina)
Cost Range: €5 (guided tour); free Sun morning at Mass
Tips: Largest dome in Spain (33m diameter — bigger than St Paul's London smaller only than St Peter's Rome and the Pantheon). Six side chapels with paintings by Zurbarán Goya (early career — San Bernardino de Siena) and Bayeu. Stand under the dome and the perspective folds in on itself. 17th-century walnut choir stalls rescued from a destroyed Segovian monastery.

Name: Real Monasterio de las Descalzas Reales
Address: Plaza de las Descalzas s/n 28013 Madrid
Cost Range: €8 (under 5 free; free Wed/Thu 16:00–18:30 for EU citizens)
Tips: Active cloistered convent founded 1559 by Juana of Austria (sister of Felipe II). Behind austere brick facade hides one of Madrid's most extraordinary art collections — Titian Rubens Brueghel the Elder. Flemish tapestries from cartoons by Rubens (only two complete sets in the world). Spanish guided tour only (audio English). Nuns still live behind cloister.

Name: Iglesia de San Antonio de los Alemanes
Tips: THE most surprising interior in Madrid. Plain brick exterior — step inside and the entire elliptical space (1633) is covered floor-to-ceiling in baroque trompe-l'œil frescoes by Luca Giordano (1689) showing life of St Anthony of Padua. Every column every cornice every panel painted. Almost no tourists. Active church. Mon–Thu 10:30–13:30 + 17:30–19:30.

Name: Iglesia de San Ginés
Tips: One of Madrid's oldest active parishes (founded 1358 rebuilt 1645). El Greco's Expulsion of the Money-Changers from the Temple hangs in the Capilla del Santísimo Cristo (ask the verger to unlock if not lit). The Chocolatería San Ginés next door is named after the church.

Name: Real Ermita de San Antonio de la Florida
Address: Glorieta de San Antonio de la Florida 5 28008 Madrid
Tips: THE Sistine Chapel of Madrid. Small neoclassical hermitage (1798) whose dome Goya painted in 6 months the same year — and where Goya himself is buried (1919 repatriation from Bordeaux; skull missing). Fresco inverts religious-painting hierarchy: angels on lower walls Madrid people in dome. CLOSED for conservation since Mar 17 2025 — verify before going.

Name: Basílica Pontificia de San Miguel
Tips: Tucked behind Mercado de San Miguel. Rare Roman-baroque convex facade (Italian style almost unknown in Spain). Interior high-ornate painted gilded. Quiet even when market is rammed. Active church.

Name: Iglesia de San Pedro el Viejo
Tips: Possibly Madrid's oldest church — 14th-century Mudéjar brick tower survives unchanged from medieval city. Interior rebuilt many times but tower is the real thing. Easy to walk past without noticing.

Name: Iglesia de San Andrés + Capilla del Obispo
Address: Plaza de San Andrés 1 28005 Madrid (La Latina)
Cost Range: Free; Capilla del Obispo by guided tour only
Tips: Twin churches sharing a plaza in La Latina. Capilla del Obispo (1535) is one of the last Gothic + earliest Renaissance buildings in Madrid — extraordinary carved alabaster altarpiece by Francisco Giralte (1547). Opened only by booked guided tour (Tue/Thu — limited slots). San Andrés next door is grand baroque (Capilla de San Isidro where Madrid's patron was originally buried). Plaza de la Paja outside is Madrid's loveliest medieval square.

Name: Cementerio de la Almudena
Address: Av. de Daroca 90 28017 Madrid (Metro La Almudena L2)
Tips: Spain's largest cemetery — 120 hectares ~5 million graves (more than current Madrid population). Opened 1884 after 1834 cholera epidemic. Long avenues of family pantheons in every style — Neoclassical Egyptian Revival Modernista Gothic Revival Art Nouveau. Resting place of Pío Baroja Joaquín Rodrigo Manolete and the heart of Saint Isidore.
Family: Good — silent fascinating for older kids

Name: Cementerio de la Sacramental de San Isidro
Address: Pg. de la Ermita del Santo 72 28011 Madrid (Carabanchel)
Tips: Madrid's most prestigious 19th-century cemetery (founded 1811). Architecturally denser than Almudena — Romantic and Modernista mausoleums. Goya was first buried here (1828 before exhumation). Dukes of Alba José Zorrilla Mariano José de Larra Federico de Madrazo. Often called Madrid's Père-Lachaise. 1842 neoclassical chapel at centre.

Name: Panteón de los Hombres Ilustres
Tips: Small neo-Byzantine pantheon (1899) attached to Atocha basilica. Spain's failed answer to Westminster Abbey. Tombs of 19th-century Spanish prime ministers (Cánovas Sagasta Canalejas) in extraordinarily carved white-marble sarcophagi by Mariano Benlliure and Agustín Querol. Almost no visitors. The Sagasta sarcophagus is one of Spain's finest funerary sculptures.

Name: El Capricho de La Alameda de Osuna
Address: Paseo de la Alameda de Osuna 25 28042 Madrid (Metro El Capricho L5)
Tips: Madrid's most beautiful park hidden in plain sight. 14-hectare 18th-century pleasure garden created for Duchess of Osuna — labyrinth maze (only one in Madrid) neoclassical pavilions artificial lake with Doric temple-on-an-island apiary Civil War bunker. Designed by Italian Pablo Boutelou. Sat–Sun + holidays only.

Name: Matadero Madrid
Tips: 1920s former municipal slaughterhouse in Arganzuela converted into sprawling cultural campus — theatre contemporary art design library vast outdoor terrace beside Madrid Río. Bricks-and-rivets architecture restored beautifully. 5 min walk from apt. Kids' programmes most weekends.

Name: Madrid Río
Address: Plaza de Legazpi 28045 Madrid (and along the river)
Tips: 10 km of riverside park along the Manzanares — opened 2011 after M-30 motorway was buried underground. 33 playgrounds urban beach skate parks art installations 1815 baroque Puente de Toledo with statues. Right next to apartment. The tobogán giant playground slides between Matadero and Marqués de Vadillo are a hit.

Name: Estación de Atocha — Tropical Garden
Address: Atocha Renfe station Pza. del Emperador Carlos V 28012 Madrid
Tips: Inside Atocha train station's former main hall (1851 restored 1992 by Rafael Moneo) is a 4000m² indoor tropical garden — 500+ plant species palms banana trees ferns a turtle pond. Free accessible without train ticket. Mostly known only to passengers.

Name: Real Jardín Botánico
Tips: Founded 1755 by Carlos III on Paseo del Prado. 8 hectares 5000+ plant species across formal terraces Linnaean rose garden tropical greenhouse pavilion (Pabellón de Villanueva) and 19th-century palmhouse. Quiet shaded full of butterflies. Bonsai Pavilion donated by Felipe González (most over 100 years old). Daily 10:00–21:00 Apr–Sep.

Name: CaixaForum Madrid + Vertical Garden
Tips: Herzog & de Meuron-designed cultural centre (2008) — former electrical substation with rusted-steel upper extension floating on recessed glass base (the brick volume appears to levitate). Across the street: 24m vertical wall garden by Patrick Blanc — 15000 plants on a vertical surface. Both free to view. Rotating art exhibitions inside.
Family: Good — kids love the green wall

Name: Mirador de Madrid (Cibeles Palace Rooftop)
Tips: Top of Palacio de Cibeles (wedding-cake city hall 1909). Panoramic rooftop terrace 360° views over Gran Vía Prado Retiro and out to Guadarrama mountains. Lift to 8th floor then climb to open terrace. One of Madrid's best views less queued than Círculo de Bellas Artes. Sunset.

Name: Círculo de Bellas Artes Rooftop
Tips: Most famous rooftop in Madrid — 1926 cultural centre with panoramic terrace looking down Gran Vía and over central Madrid. Sunset queues. Pair with a Gran Vía walk.

Name: Faro de Moncloa
Tips: 92m glass-and-steel viewing tower (1992) in Moncloa. Lift up to glassed observation level — highest publicly accessible viewpoint in Madrid. Sweeping views over western half of the city Casa de Campo Guadarrama range. Almost no tourists. Weekday morning <5 min wait. Combine with Museo de América next door.

Name: Lavapiés Street-Art Walk
Address: Centred on Plaza de Lavapiés 28012 Madrid
Tips: Madrid's most multicultural neighbourhood is canvas for the annual C.A.L.L.E. street-art festival (every Sep — running during this trip!). Murals on C/ del Ave María C/ Doctor Fourquet C/ Embajadores. International artists work changes year by year. Combine with Tabacalera (giant former tobacco factory cultural centre) and Reina Sofía.

Name: Mercado de San Miguel
Tips: Stunning 1916 wrought-iron and glass market hall next to Plaza Mayor — restored 2009 as gourmet food hall (~20 stalls). More beautiful than Barcelona's La Boqueria less chaotic slightly more expensive. Best before noon or after 22:00. Eat standing at central marble counters.

Name: Convento del Corpus Christi (Las Carboneras)
Address: Plaza del Conde de Miranda 3 28005 Madrid
Tips: 17th-century cloistered convent of Hieronymite nuns. The famous Madrid 'turnstile sweets' — ring bell unseen nun answers through wooden screen you order pastries put money in revolving turnstile biscuits emerge. Nuns and you never see each other. Surreal hidden intensely Madrid. Good pastas de almendra + mantecadas.

Name: Casa Museo Lope de Vega
Tips: House where Spain's greatest playwright Lope de Vega lived 1610–1635 and wrote ~500 of his ~1500 plays — preserved nearly unchanged. 17th-century domestic Madrid: kitchen with original tile study with goose-quill pens small garden where Lope grew vegetables. Free guided visit (45 min multilingual). Book online ahead.

Name: Casa de la Panadería (Plaza Mayor)
Tips: Only original building of Plaza Mayor — north facade in stunning early 20th-century allegorical frescoes (re-painted 1992). The bakers' guild house. Daylight illumination is best.
